steps.title: Understand what the blank must mean
steps.content: The passage explains a speed difference between running and flying by saying that the features [blank] for running restrict top speeds more than the features needed for flying. The blank parallels the word “needed” in the second half of the comparison. The blank must therefore mean something close to “required” or “necessary.”

steps.title: Test each word against that meaning
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘essential’: Means “absolutely necessary” — directly parallels “needed” and explains why running’s required features impose a greater speed cost. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘trivial’: Means “unimportant” — contradicts the passage’s logic, which attributes real speed-restricting force to these features. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘challenging’: Means “difficult” — describes a quality of the features themselves, not their necessity for running; does not explain the speed restriction. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘approved’: Means “officially sanctioned” — irrelevant to a biological explanation of speed differences. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
